dependencies: Reliance on suppliers for equipment, components, and services including finished products, electronic components, and commodities
Single-source supplier relationships for certain components where alternative sources are not available or relationship is advantageous
Supply chain can introduce security vulnerabilities into technologies across the industry
risks: Shortages of components, commodities, or other materials affecting manufacturing efficiencies and timely delivery
Changes in cost of purchases due to inflation, exchange rate fluctuations, taxes, tariffs, commodity market volatility
Poor quality or insecure supply chain affecting reliability and reputation
Embargoes, sanctions, and other trade restrictions affecting ability to purchase from various suppliers
Unavailability or delivery delays for single-source components or products
Malware attacks could spread throughout supply chain via authorized network connections
Software supply chains can introduce security vulnerabilities

regulatory_risks: Corporate income tax regulations including OECD BEPS Pillar Two minimum 15% tax rate
Environmental regulations including EU Eco-design for Sustainable Products Regulation
Material, product, certification, and labeling laws and regulations
Privacy and cybersecurity laws including EU Cyber Resiliency Act
FDA approval requirements (implied for Life Sciences customers)
Trade controls, tariffs, embargoes affecting business activities
Compliance with environmental requirements for discharge of substances, disposal of hazardous wastes
Contract and intellectual property rights enforcement challenges in certain countries
ESG reporting requirements with potential regulatory penalties for inaccurate or incomplete disclosures
Taxation policies in over 100 countries where company operates

sanction_risks: Embargoes, sanctions, and other trade restrictions affecting supplier relationships
Compliance with existing and future export/import regulations
Economic, political, regulatory risks in emerging markets restricting ability to exchange, transact, or pay dividends with foreign currencies
Political instability in various global jurisdictions
Enforcement challenges in certain countries regarding contracts and intellectual property
Prevalence of corruption in certain countries
